sections.paragraph:
συγκόπτω fut. συγκόψω; 1 aor. συνέκοψα LXX; 2 aor. pass. συνεκόπην (Eur., Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; TestJud 3:4; SibOr 3, 188; 613)

① to break into pieces, break (X., Cyr. 6, 4, 3; PSI 630, 20; 4 Km 24:13) stones Hv 3, 6, 1.

② to bring an end to someth. as an effective force, destroy, fig. ext. of 1 (Lucian, Cal. 1) τὴν δύναμιν τοῦ διαβόλου break the power of the devil Hm 12, 6, 4.

③ to be crushed inwardly, be overcome, fig. ext. of 1, pass. intr. sense of a depressed frame of mind ἀπὸ τῆς λύπης by grief Hv 5:4.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Hv Hv = Visions

someth. someth. = something

ext. ext. = extension

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

intr. intr. = intransitive

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 953
